metadata.name: UTTSAV Rulebook
metadata.scope: Portal UI guidance + India event-permission compliance references
metadata.legal_note: Guidance only. Final approvals and conditions are decided by competent local authorities.

documents.title: Portal Step 1: Event Details
documents.content: Step 1 requires eventName, eventType, crowdSize, startDate, endDate, startTime and endTime. Missing fields block forward movement. Crowd size influences risk analysis and department routing.

documents.title: Portal Step 2: Venue Details
documents.content: Step 2 requires venueName, venueType, address, city, wardNumber and venueOwnership. Map location (latitude/longitude/address) should be captured via the map picker. If venue ownership is Venue Booking, venue owner consent is required.

documents.title: Portal Step 3: Infrastructure
documents.content: Step 3 captures infrastructure toggles: stageRequired, soundSystem, temporaryStructures, foodStalls and fireworks. These fields impact both risk score and department routing.

documents.title: Portal Step 4: Safety
documents.content: Step 4 requires securityPersonnelCount. Medical facility, first-aid and ambulance toggles strengthen preparedness. CrowdManagementPlan upload becomes required when expected crowd exceeds 500.

documents.title: Portal Step 5: Traffic Impact
documents.content: Step 5 requires parkingCapacity and trafficImpact. If roadClosureRequired is true, traffic police clearance and route planning are expected before final approval.

documents.title: Portal Step 6: Waste Management
documents.content: Step 6 collects wasteDisposalPlan, cleaningContractor and numberOfDustbins. For large events these fields are mandatory. Waste planning reduces rejection risk for public-ground and food-stall events.

documents.title: Portal Step 7: Documents
documents.content: Step 7 enforces required uploads. Organizer ID Proof is always required. Venue Owner Consent is required for booked venues. Crowd Management Plan is required above 500 attendees. Insurance Certificate is required for large events.

documents.title: Noise Rules: Loudspeaker Permissions and Time Restrictions
documents.content: Under the Noise Pollution (Regulation and Control) Rules, 2000, loudspeakers/public address systems require written permission from the competent authority. Night-time use (10:00 PM to 6:00 AM) is generally restricted, with limited state-notified festive exemptions up to 12:00 midnight under conditions.

source.label: Central Pollution Control Board (Noise Rules text)

documents.title: Food Stalls: FSSAI Licensing/Registration via FoSCoS
documents.content: When food stalls are part of an event, organizers and participating food business operators should ensure valid FSSAI licensing/registration as applicable. The official compliance platform is FoSCoS.

documents.title: Fireworks: Explosives Rule Compliance (PESO Reference)
documents.content: Fireworks use should comply with Explosives Rules and only authorized categories/conditions. Event organizers should coordinate approvals from local police/fire authorities and comply with PESO-referenced safety constraints, including permitted items and sound limits.

documents.title: Mass Gathering Safety Planning (NDMA Guide)
documents.content: NDMA crowd-management guidance emphasizes proactive planning, hazard/risk assessment, clear stakeholder roles, emergency response readiness, and organizer accountability at mass gatherings. Organizers should maintain crowd management and emergency evacuation plans for high-footfall events.

documents.title: Public Rally/Meeting/Procession Permission
documents.content: For rallies, processions, public meetings and amplified sound use, organizers should obtain permission from the competent local authority (typically Commissioner of Police or District Magistrate). Conditions can include route control, timing, public order safeguards and liability obligations.

source.label: Maharashtra State Police FAQ + Mumbai Procession Terms

documents.title: Road Closure / Traffic Movement Permissions
documents.content: If an event affects roads or route movement, organizers should apply to the relevant traffic police authority for entry/diversion permissions and comply with imposed route/time conditions before event date.

documents.title: Temporary Structures/Pandals Fire Precautions
documents.content: Temporary structures should follow fire safety precautions such as adequate exits, emergency lighting, non-combustible separation near kitchens, and controls on fireworks/open flames near structure boundaries. Event venues should have valid fire NOC/FSC where applicable.

source.label: Delhi Fire Service Temporary Structures Guidance

documents.title: Disaster Management Framework and Authority Roles
documents.content: The Disaster Management Act, 2005 establishes national/state/district responsibilities for preparedness, mitigation and response. Large events should align with district-level disaster management expectations and emergency coordination norms.

source.label: Ministry of Home Affairs - Disaster Management Act, 2005

documents.title: Waste Segregation and Municipal Compliance
documents.content: Event waste plans should include source segregation, collection logistics and handover to local systems, aligned with Solid Waste Management Rules and municipal bye-laws. Larger public events should define cleanup responsibility and contractor accountability.

source.label: MoEFCC publication referencing Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016
